name: CERT: Risk Assessed
description: Every finding is risk-assessed on the three CERT factors: severity, likelihood, and remediation cost.
rationale: SEI CERT risk assessment: each guideline/finding is scored on Severity (low/medium/high), Likelihood (unlikely/probable/likely), and Remediation Cost (high/medium/low).
source: SEI CERT Coding Standards (Carnegie Mellon Software Engineering Institute) — CERT C and CERT C++ Secure Coding Standards, with the CERT risk-assessment methodology (Severity × Likelihood × Remediation Cost → Priority → Level L1/L2/L3). https://wiki.sei.cmu.edu/confluence/display/seccode
